response.justification: Wei Chen is a strong senior individual contributor with broad full-stack and some high-throughput distributed-systems experience, but the profile does not meet the executive bar for this role. There is no evidence of senior technical leadership, scaling engineering organizations from 10 to 30+ people, or building and operating production multi-agent AI systems in regulated environments. AI exposure is limited to recent MCP tooling and agent-infrastructure contributions, and fintech exposure (RIDE Capital) was as an IC. The gap between a hands-on craftsman profile and a CTO mandate covering agentic architecture, credit-risk systems, and board communication is too wide.
strengths: High-throughput distributed-systems experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Recent hands-on exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq
Strong engineering-craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, teaching/workshops)
concerns: No executive or people-leadership experience — no track record scaling teams or developing technical-leadership layers
No demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems or LLM evaluation/fine-tuning depth
No experience operating AI systems in regulated financial environments or building compliance/auditability infrastructure

key_factors.factor: Lack of senior technical leadership / team-scaling track record
No production multi-agent AI or regulated-fintech AI experience
Solid distributed-systems and recent agentic-tooling exposure
